article.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant to Replace Default ChatGPT Model with Fewer Hallucinations
article.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Instant becomes the new default for all ChatGPT users, claiming 52.5% fewer hallucinations on high-stakes prompts in medicine, law and finance while adding memory source transparency and maintaining low latency.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 Instant, a new foundation model that is replacing GPT-5.3 Instant as the default ChatGPT model for all users. The company says the update delivers significant improvements in factuality while keeping the low latency that made previous Instant models popular for everyday use, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/05/openai-releases-gpt-5-5-instant-a-new-default-model-for-chatgpt/).

The rollout began on May 5, 2026, and reaches free and paid ChatGPT users immediately. GPT-5.3 Instant remains available as an option for three months before retirement, giving users time to adjust, as reported by [The Verge](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/924225/openai-chatgpt-default-model-gpt-5-5-instant).

## What We Know

- **Hallucination reductions**: Based on internal evaluations, GPT-5.5 Instant produced "52.5% fewer hallucinated claims" than GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes prompts covering medicine, law, and finance. It also "reduced inaccurate claims by 37.3% on especially challenging conversations users had flagged for factual errors," according to [The Verge](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/924225/openai-chatgpt-default-model-gpt-5-5-instant). OpenAI describes the changes as "significant improvements in factuality across the board."

- **Performance benchmarks**: The new model scored 81.2 on the AIME 2025 math test, up from 65.4 for the prior Instant model. On the MMMU-Pro multimodal reasoning benchmark it achieved 76, compared with 69.2 previously, as detailed by [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/05/openai-releases-gpt-5-5-instant-a-new-default-model-for-chatgpt/).

- **Context and personalization**: GPT-5.5 Instant can use its search tool to pull from past conversations, uploaded files, and Gmail for more personalized answers. The enhanced personalization feature launches first for Plus and Pro users on the web, with mobile support "coming soon" and expansion to Free, Go, Business, and Enterprise users planned in the coming weeks, per both [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/05/openai-releases-gpt-5-5-instant-a-new-default-model-for-chatgpt/) and [The Verge](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/924225/openai-chatgpt-default-model-gpt-5-5-instant).

- **Memory sources transparency**: Across all ChatGPT models, a new "memory sources" feature now shows users the specific context (previous chats, Gmail, files) used to generate personalized responses. Users can delete outdated sources or correct them. When a chat is shared, recipients cannot see the memory sources, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/05/openai-releases-gpt-5-5-instant-a-new-default-model-for-chatgpt/).

- **Response style changes**: GPT-5.5 Instant produces "tighter and more to-the-point" answers and avoids "gratuitous emojis," while becoming "more capable across everyday tasks" such as analyzing image uploads and deciding when to search the web, [The Verge](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/924225/openai-chatgpt-default-model-gpt-5-5-instant) reported.

- **Developer access**: Through the API, the GPT-5.5 family is available as "chat-latest." The previous GPT-5.3 remains selectable for paid API users for three months, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/05/openai-releases-gpt-5-5-instant-a-new-default-model-for-chatgpt/).

The full GPT-5.5 model (non-Instant) launched in late April 2026 with gains in coding, knowledge work, and agentic capabilities. GPT-5.5 Instant is presented as the fast, reliable daily driver built on that foundation.

## What We Don't Know

- The precise methodology and dataset behind OpenAI's internal hallucination evaluations have not been fully disclosed publicly, though the company published a system card detailing some of the testing approach.

- User reaction to the retirement of GPT-5.3 Instant remains to be seen. Past model deprecations, such as the GPT-4o retirement earlier in 2026, drew significant backlash from users who had formed attachments to specific model "personalities."

- The timeline for complete rollout of enhanced personalization and memory sources features across all tiers (especially Free and Enterprise) has not been specified beyond "soon" and "in the coming weeks."

- Independent third-party benchmarks comparing GPT-5.5 Instant directly against rivals on the same hallucination or factuality tests are not yet available.

## Analysis

The release continues OpenAI's pattern of maintaining a fast default "Instant" model for broad ChatGPT usage alongside more powerful reasoning-focused variants. By pairing quantified hallucination reductions with new transparency tools like memory sources, the company is directly addressing two of the most persistent criticisms of consumer AI chatbots: factual reliability in professional contexts and opaque personalization.

The decision to keep the prior default available for a three-month transition period shows some learning from earlier model retirement controversies. Whether the measured improvements in factuality and the added user controls will be enough to satisfy power users—and whether the low-latency promise holds under real-world load—will become clearer as adoption widens over the next several weeks.

For developers, the "chat-latest" alias provides a stable way to track the current recommended model without hard-coding version strings, a practical concession amid OpenAI's rapid release cadence.
